project.name: AgentSpend
project.tagline: Budget-aware AI orchestration with approval gates

description.title: The problem it solves
description.content: AI agents are great at taking action, but terrible at respecting budgets by default. Once a workflow starts, it can chain together search, scraping, and model calls with little visibility or control. Teams need a way to estimate cost before execution, block risky tasks, require approvals, and audit what happened afterward. That is the problem AgentSpend solves.
description.subtitle: describe what can people use it for, or how it makes existing tasks easier/safer e.t.c (markdown supported)

description.title: Challenges we ran into
description.content: The biggest challenge was making AI workflow execution feel controlled instead of opaque. We had to estimate cost before execution, manage multiple task states like blocked/approval/running/failed, and keep each step auditable. We solved this by simplifying the orchestration flow, defining explicit state transitions, and logging execution metadata so every task could be reviewed and debugged clearly.
description.subtitle: tell us about any specific bug or hurdle you ran into while building this project. how did you get over it? (markdown supported)

prize_tracks.name: Using PayWithLocus.com to leverage our suite.
prize_tracks.description:
Building systems where AI agents can buy/sell services from each other, or build consumer-facing AI agents that can make purchases and transactions, or create AI agents that handle real business financial operations

• Example: AI agents trading compute resources, data processing services, API calls
• Example: Personal shopping agents, bookings bots, subscription management
• Examples: Automated procurement agents, expense management bots, invoice processing
• Examples: Something else cool!

More info in our discord: discord.gg/locus

project_tracks.description: AgentSpend is directly aligned with the PayWithLocus track because our product is built around controlled AI task execution. We use Locus to support a payment-aware workflow layer where tasks can be evaluated, gated, and executed with better spending visibility. This fits our core goal: preventing uncontrolled AI spend through estimation, approvals, and traceable execution.

project_commits.commit: AgentSpend MVP with cost controls and approvals
